title: ClusterIP Service for Internal Communication
description:
Deploy an Nginx backend Deployment and expose it with a ClusterIP Service. Then verify a second Pod can reach the backend using the Service DNS name — not the Pod IP.

**Why this matters**: ClusterIP is the default and most common Service type. It gives your app a stable internal hostname that survives Pod restarts, enabling reliable service-to-service calls inside the cluster.

**Common Pitfalls**: selector labels on the Service must exactly match the Deployment Pod template labels — a single typo creates an empty Endpoints list and silent connection failures.
steps: Complete the Deployment manifest: set app=backend label on Pod template, use image nginx:alpine, expose containerPort 80
Complete the ClusterIP Service manifest: set selector app=backend, port 80 targeting containerPort 80
Add a curl client Pod that runs 'sleep 3600' so you can exec into it
Apply the manifest with kubectl apply -f solution.yaml
Verify the Service has endpoints: kubectl get endpoints backend-svc
Exec into the curl pod and run: curl http://backend-svc — expect an HTML response
hints: A ClusterIP Service is the default; you can omit 'type: ClusterIP' or write it explicitly
Set the label value to 'backend' on the Deployment pod template and match it exactly in the Service selector
The Service selector must use the same key-value pair as the Pod template labels in the Deployment
DNS short names work within the same namespace — 'backend-svc' resolves without the full FQDN
Use 'kubectl get endpoints backend-svc' before testing connectivity to confirm Pods are registered

editor_config.initial_code:
# --- Backend Deployment ---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: backend
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: # TODO: use 'backend'
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: # TODO: use 'backend'
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: backend
        image: # TODO: lightweight nginx image
        ports:
        - containerPort: # TODO: HTTP port
---
# --- ClusterIP Service ---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: backend-svc
spec:
  # type: ClusterIP is the default — can be omitted
  selector:
    app: # TODO: use 'backend'
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: # TODO: must match containerPort above
---
# --- Curl client Pod ---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: curl-client
spec:
  containers:
  - name: curl
    image: curlimages/curl:latest
    command: ["sleep", "3600"]

_solution.code_solution:
# --- Backend Deployment ---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: backend
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: backend
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: backend
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: backend
        image: nginx:alpine
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
---
# --- ClusterIP Service ---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: backend-svc
spec:
  selector:
    app: backend
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 80
---
# --- Curl client Pod ---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: curl-client
spec:
  containers:
  - name: curl
    image: curlimages/curl:latest
    command: ["sleep", "3600"]
_solution.validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

kubectl apply -f solution.yaml

echo "Waiting for backend Deployment to be ready..."
kubectl rollout status deployment/backend --timeout=90s
if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "✗ backend Deployment did not become ready"
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ backend Deployment is ready"

echo "Waiting for curl-client Pod to be running..."
timeout=60
elapsed=0
while [ $elapsed -lt $timeout ]; do
  phase=$(kubectl get pod curl-client -o jsonpath='{.status.phase}' 2>/dev/null || echo "Pending")
  if [ "$phase" = "Running" ]; then
    break
  fi
  sleep 3
  ((elapsed+=3))
done
if [ "$phase" != "Running" ]; then
  echo "✗ curl-client Pod did not reach Running state"
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ curl-client Pod is running"

ENDPOINTS=$(kubectl get endpoints backend-svc -o jsonpath='{.subsets[0].addresses}' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
if [ -z "$ENDPOINTS" ] || [ "$ENDPOINTS" = "null" ]; then
  echo "✗ Service backend-svc has no endpoints — check selector labels match Deployment Pod template labels"
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ Service backend-svc has endpoints"

HTTP_CODE=$(kubectl exec curl-client -- curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://backend-svc 2>/dev/null || echo "000")
if [ "$HTTP_CODE" = "200" ]; then
  echo "✓ curl-client successfully reached backend-svc via ClusterIP DNS (HTTP $HTTP_CODE)"
  exit 0
else
  echo "✗ HTTP request to backend-svc returned code $HTTP_CODE (expected 200)"
  exit 1
fi
